links.note: The explicit issue-side document reference matched the reviewed document lineage. This proves a shared lineage, not an exact version match or a direct Review-to-Issue relationship.

governance_checks.statement: Reviewed document version equals the expected CDE version.
governance_checks.note: VERSION_1 was obtained from the Reviews endpoint, but no independent CDE item/version lookup was performed during this run.

governance_checks.statement: Review belongs to the expected project.
governance_checks.note: REVIEW_1 was retrieved through the project-scoped Reviews API chain for PROJECT_1.
REVIEW_1 returned WORKFLOW_1 and its two-step workflow definition.

governance_checks.statement: Review progress and outcome are internally consistent.
governance_checks.note: Current-step correlation was an exact identifier match to ordinal 2, and the independent approval endpoint returned IN_REVIEW.

governance_checks.statement: Issue belongs to the same project.
governance_checks.note: ISSUE_1 was retrieved through the same project-scoped Issues API chain.

governance_checks.statement: Issue type and status are available.
governance_checks.note: Issue type and status were independently observed.

governance_checks.statement: A file/review/issue relationship is positively verified as a shared document lineage.
governance_checks.note: ISSUE_1's explicit lineage reference exactly matched DOCUMENT_1.

governance_checks.statement: The issue status is not assumed from the review outcome.
governance_checks.note: Issue status came from the Issues API and was not inferred from the review.

governance_checks.statement: The review outcome is not assumed from the issue status.
governance_checks.note: Review approval outcome came from the version-scoped approval endpoint and was not inferred from issue status.
A separate issue-resolution or response document was not traced in this execution.

warnings.message: The target EMEA project was discovered successfully; non-target regional discovery probes returned authorization warnings.
The Reviews responses did not provide an explicit authoritative service generation.
REVIEW_1 selection used an exact and unique designator in the review display name because the listing exposed no stronger scenario discriminator.
VERSION_1 was captured from the review's reviewed-version collection and was not independently retrieved through the general CDE item/version tools.
The reviewed-version record supplied null approval_status while the dedicated version-scoped approval endpoint supplied IN_REVIEW.
No Relationships API records were returned for ISSUE_1. This verifies the lookup capability but does not prove that ISSUE_1 was unrelated in the project or Autodesk UI.
The issue reference matched DOCUMENT_1 at lineage level; an exact match to VERSION_1 was not established.
The evidence establishes a shared document lineage, not a direct Review-to-Issue relationship.
A separate response or resolution document was not observed during this run.
Sanitised observations were transferred through the conversation and structured locally without persisting live identifiers in the repository.
